{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)}} [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)}} [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)}} [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)}} [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. {{基礎情報 武士 | 氏名 = 源 頼国 | 画像 = 源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = 江戸時代に描かれた源頼国の想像図（『絵本頼光一代記』） | 時代 = [[平安時代]]中期 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = [[天喜]]元年（[[1058年]]）? | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 霊名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[正四位|正四位下]]、[[蔵人]]、[[左衛門尉]]、[[讃岐国#国司|讃岐守]]、[[美濃国#国司|美濃守]] | 幕府 = | 主君 = [[藤原彰子]]（→[[後一条天皇|敦成親王]]） | 藩 = | 氏族 = [[清和源氏]]流[[摂津源氏]]系[[多田源氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[源頼光]]
母：[[藤原元平]]の娘 | 兄弟 = '''頼国'''、[[源頼家 (摂津源氏)|頼家]]、[[源頼基|頼基]]、[[永壽]]、[[頼昭]]、[[源済政]]室、[[大江公資]]室、[[藤原道綱]]室、[[源資通]]室
養兄弟：''[[源頼平|頼平]]''、''[[源頼明|頼明]]''、''[[源頼貞|頼貞]]''、''[[源頼範|頼範]]''、''[[源孝道|孝道]]''、''[[相模 (歌人)|相模]]'' | 妻 = [[藤原信理]]の娘、[[藤原師長 (備後守)|藤原師長]]の娘、[[藤原中清]]の娘など | 子 = [[源頼弘|頼弘]]、[[源頼資|頼資]]、[[源頼実|頼実]]、[[源実国|実国]]、'''[[源頼綱|頼綱]]'''、[[六条斎院宣旨]]、[[源国房|国房]]、[[源頼仲 (摂津源氏)|頼仲]]、[[源師光 (清和源氏)|師光]]、[[源頼房 (平安時代中期)|頼房]]、[[源頼任 (平安時代中期)|頼任]]、[[源富国|富国]]、[[源明円|明円]]、[[藤原為房]]室、[[藤原師実]]室、[[藤原顕家 (小野宮流)|藤原顕家]]室、[[藤原良綱]]室、[[藤原定輔]]室、[[藤原盛綱]]室 | 特記事項 = }} '''源 頼国'''（みなもと の よりくに）は、[[平安時代]]中期の[[武将]]・[[官人]]。[[源頼光]]の長男『[[尊卑分脈]]』{{Cite web |title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-03}}。[[いとこ]]に[[源頼義]]がいる。 == 経歴 == 父・頼光同様、主に[[京都|京]]における中級官人として活動する。特に[[藤原道長]]一族との結びつきが強く、道長の娘[[藤原彰子|上東門院彰子]]、その所生の皇子敦成親王（[[後一条天皇]]）に長きに渡って近侍した。武人としてよりも文人としての活動が主体であり、[[寛弘]]4年（[[1007年]]）に道長の催した[[漢詩]]の会に出席したことなどが記録に見える。[[内昇殿]]を許され、娘達を[[摂政]][[藤原師実]]、[[参議]][[藤原為房]]といった[[公卿]]の妻妾に配すなど、総じて貴族社会の一員としての側面が強かった。 説話上では、父頼光の[[酒呑童子]]退治に[[頼光四天王|四天王]]や[[藤原保昌]]らと共に同行し、[[茨木童子]]の住居であった「鬼ヶ城」を攻め落とす武士として描かれるが、史実上の頼国に武人としての目立った事跡はなく、[[寛仁]]3年（[[1019年]]）に暴漢を逮捕したことなど、主に京周辺の[[警察]]的活動に従事した。 [[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]の長昌寺の[[大般若波羅蜜多経]]に以下の奥書をしている{{Cite web |title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-08-03}}{{Sfn|横山|2024|p=15}}。 {{Quotation|維時　長元五年
　皇都鎮護将
　　　従五位下摂津守源朝臣
奉三宝弟子　　頼国謹書、}} == 系譜 == *父：[[源頼光]] *母：[[藤原元平]]の娘 *妻：[[藤原信理]]の娘 **長男：[[源頼弘]] - 従五位下讃岐守、出家 **三男：[[源頼実]]（1015-1044） **四男：[[源実国]]（1020-?） *妻：[[藤原師長 (備後守)|藤原師長]]の娘 **次男：[[源頼資]]（?-1066） *妻：[[藤原仲清]]の娘 **五男：[[源頼綱]]（1025-1097） **八男：[[源師光 (清和源氏)|源師光]]（?-1100） *妻：不詳 **六男：[[源国房]]（?-1119） **七男：[[源頼仲 (摂津源氏)|源頼仲]] **九男：[[源頼房 (平安時代中期)|源頼房]] **十男：[[源頼任 (平安時代中期)|源頼任]] **男子：[[源富国]] **男子：[[源明円]] **女子：[[六条斎院宣旨]]（?-1092） **女子：[[藤原為房]]室 **女子：[[藤原師実]]室 **女子：[[藤原顕家 (小野宮流)|藤原顕家]]室 **女子：[[藤原良綱]]室 **女子：[[藤原定輔]]室 **女子：[[藤原盛綱]]室 多くの子女に恵まれた。頼実、頼綱、師光は[[歌人]]としての活動が知られるほか、頼資、頼綱、国房、師光らの子孫が後代武門として様々な形で歴史に名を刻んでいる。また娘達のうち、師実妻は[[左大臣]][[藤原家忠|家忠]]（[[花山院家]]祖）、為房妻は[[参議]][[藤原為隆|為隆]]（[[吉田家]]祖）、[[権中納言]][[藤原顕隆|顕隆]]（[[葉室家]]祖）らをそれぞれ生んだことで知られ、さらに別の娘の一人は歌人「[[六条斎院宣旨]]」として名を残している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=美濃土岐氏―平安から戦国を駆け抜けた本宗家の戦い |date=2024-4-10 |year=2024 |publisher=戎光祥出版株式会社 |ref=harv |author=横山住雄 |last=横山 |first=住雄 |author-link=横山住雄 |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == 関連項目 == *[[摂津源氏]] *[[多田源氏]] *[[美濃源氏]] *[[丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:みなもと の よりくに}} [[Category:摂津源氏|よりくに]] [[Category:多田源氏|よりくに]] [[Category:源頼光の子女|よりくに]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:平安時代の武士]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:1058年没]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=draft}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Settsu Genji]] [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period aristocrats]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children.
