{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Tada Genji]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]]

Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. {{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:多田源氏]]

For the protection of the capital

Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. {{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:平安時代の武士]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Heian-period samurai]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[en:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

{{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|qq=}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Minamoto no Yorikuni]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:多田源氏]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:摂津源氏]] [[Category:源頼光の子女]]

Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children.
