[[ja:源頼国]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|death_date=1058|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Fujiwara no Nakakiyo's daughter|WD=Q106385481}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Noburi's daughter (Minamoto no Yorikuni's wife)|WD=Q110382612}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Kunifusa|WD=Q11564033}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q106385754}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q119381212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Sanekuni|WD=Q11564054}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorihiro|WD=Q11564353}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Morozane's wife)|WD=Q106398212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Tamefusa's wife)|WD=Q106398171}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Teisuke's wife)|WD=Q106422258}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Yoshitsuna's wife)|WD=Q110571927}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisuke|WD=Q24886478}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritsuna|WD=Q11564359}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorizane|WD=Q11564350}}; {{ill|Q106421877|WD=Q106421877}}; {{ill|Q106422454|WD=Q106422454}}; {{ill|Q110572285|WD=Q110572285}}; {{ill|Q110572852|WD=Q110572852}}; {{ill|Rokujō Saiin no Senji|WD=Q11392412}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Mototada's daughter (Minamoto no Yorimitsu's wife)|WD=Q110571388}}; {{ill|Taira no Korenaka's adopted daughter|WD=Q106305919}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11564344]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorikuni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}}

Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}}

[[ja:源頼国]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[ja:源頼国]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|death_date=1058|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Fujiwara no Nakakiyo's daughter|WD=Q106385481}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Noburi's daughter (Minamoto no Yorikuni's wife)|WD=Q110382612}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Kunifusa|WD=Q11564033}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q106385754}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q119381212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Sanekuni|WD=Q11564054}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorihiro|WD=Q11564353}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Morozane's wife)|WD=Q106398212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Tamefusa's wife)|WD=Q106398171}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Teisuke's wife)|WD=Q106422258}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Yoshitsuna's wife)|WD=Q110571927}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisuke|WD=Q24886478}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritsuna|WD=Q11564359}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorizane|WD=Q11564350}}; {{ill|Q106421877|WD=Q106421877}}; {{ill|Q106422454|WD=Q106422454}}; {{ill|Q110572285|WD=Q110572285}}; {{ill|Q110572852|WD=Q110572852}}; {{ill|Rokujō Saiin no Senji|WD=Q11392412}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Mototada's daughter (Minamoto no Yorimitsu's wife)|WD=Q110571388}}; {{ill|Taira no Korenaka's adopted daughter|WD=Q106305919}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11564344]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorikuni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:源頼国]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}}

| Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]

Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:源頼国]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[ja:源頼国]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|death_date=1058|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Fujiwara no Nakakiyo's daughter|WD=Q106385481}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Noburi's daughter (Minamoto no Yorikuni's wife)|WD=Q110382612}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Kunifusa|WD=Q11564033}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q106385754}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q119381212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Sanekuni|WD=Q11564054}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorihiro|WD=Q11564353}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Morozane's wife)|WD=Q106398212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Tamefusa's wife)|WD=Q106398171}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Teisuke's wife)|WD=Q106422258}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Yoshitsuna's wife)|WD=Q110571927}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisuke|WD=Q24886478}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritsuna|WD=Q11564359}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorizane|WD=Q11564350}}; {{ill|Q106421877|WD=Q106421877}}; {{ill|Q106422454|WD=Q106422454}}; {{ill|Q110572285|WD=Q110572285}}; {{ill|Q110572852|WD=Q110572852}}; {{ill|Rokujō Saiin no Senji|WD=Q11392412}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Mototada's daughter (Minamoto no Yorimitsu's wife)|WD=Q110571388}}; {{ill|Taira no Korenaka's adopted daughter|WD=Q106305919}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11564344]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorikuni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→[[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Prince Atsunari]]) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]
Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] middle-Ranking [[:en:Courtier|Courtier]] and [[:en:Samurai|Samurai]]. He was the eldest son of [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]]''[[:en:Sonpi Bunmyaku|Sonpi Bunmyaku]]'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ([[:en:Emperor Go-Ichijō|Emperor Go-Ichijō]]). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese poetry]] gathering hosted by Michinaga in [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of [[:en:Shuten-dōji|Shuten-dōji]], alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of [[:en:Ibaraki-dōji|Ibaraki-dōji]]. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in [[:en:Kannin|Kannin]] 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] * Mother: Daughter of [[:en:Fujiwara no Motohira|Fujiwara no Motohira]] * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their [[:en:Waka|Waka]] poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]

[[ja:源頼国]] [[ja:源頼国]] [[ja:源頼国]] {{Infobox person|image=File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|death_date=1058|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|spouse={{ill|Fujiwara no Nakakiyo's daughter|WD=Q106385481}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Noburi's daughter (Minamoto no Yorikuni's wife)|WD=Q110382612}}|children={{ill|Minamoto no Kunifusa|WD=Q11564033}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q106385754}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Moromitsu|WD=Q119381212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Sanekuni|WD=Q11564054}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorihiro|WD=Q11564353}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Morozane's wife)|WD=Q106398212}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Tamefusa's wife)|WD=Q106398171}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Teisuke's wife)|WD=Q106422258}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorikuni's daughter (Fujiwara no Yoshitsuna's wife)|WD=Q110571927}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorisuke|WD=Q24886478}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritsuna|WD=Q11564359}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yorizane|WD=Q11564350}}; {{ill|Q106421877|WD=Q106421877}}; {{ill|Q106422454|WD=Q106422454}}; {{ill|Q110572285|WD=Q110572285}}; {{ill|Q110572852|WD=Q110572852}}; {{ill|Rokujō Saiin no Senji|WD=Q11392412}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Mototada's daughter (Minamoto no Yorimitsu's wife)|WD=Q110571388}}; {{ill|Taira no Korenaka's adopted daughter|WD=Q106305919}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11564344]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Yorikuni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox warrior | Name = Minamoto no Yorikuni (源 頼国) | image = [[File:源頼国 Minamoto no Yorikuni 絵本頼光一代記.jpg|250px]] | Caption = Imagined portrait of Minamoto no Yorikuni from the Edo period (from ''Ehon Raikō Ichidaiki'') | Era = Mid {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} | Birth = Unknown | Death = [[Tengi (era)|Tengi]] 1 (1058)? | Alternate name = | Titles = [[Jushi'i|Jushi'i no Ge]], [[Kurōdo]], [[Saemon no Jō]], [[Governor of Sanuki Province|Sanuki no Kami]], [[Governor of Mino Province|Mino no Kami]] | Allegiance = [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Shōshi, Empress Jōtōmon'in]] (→{{ill|Emperor Go-Ichijō|en|Emperor Go-Ichijō|WD=Q374087|lt=Prince Atsunari}}) | Clan = [[Seiwa Genji]] ([[Settsu Genji]] branch of the [[Tada Genji]]) | Parents = Father: {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}}
Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Fujiwara no Motohira|en|Fujiwara no Motohira|WD=Q5098972|lt=Fujiwara no Motohira}} | Siblings = '''Yorikuni''', [[Minamoto no Yorie (Settsu Genji)|Yorie]], [[Minamoto no Yorimoto (Settsu Genji)|Yorimoto]], [[Eijū]], [[Raiyō]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Jōsei]], Wife of [[Ōe no Kōshi]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Michitsuna]], Wife of [[Minamoto no Motomichi]]
Foster siblings: ''[[Minamoto no Raihei|Raihei]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raiaki|Raiaki]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raison|Raison]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Raifumi|Raifumi]]'', ''[[Minamoto no Kōdō|Kōdō]]'', ''[[Sagami (poet)|Sagami]]'' | Spouse = Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]], daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]], among others | Children = [[Minamoto no Yorihiro|Yorihiro]], [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu|Yoriyasu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritane|Yoritane]], [[Minamoto no Koremichi|Koremichi]], '''[[Minamoto no Yoritō|Yoritō]]''', [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], [[Minamoto no Kunifusa|Kunifusa]], [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]], [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]], [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]], [[Minamoto no Tomifusa|Tomifusa]], [[Minamoto no Myōen|Myōen]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]], Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] | Notes = }} '''Minamoto no Yorikuni''' (源 頼国) was a {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} middle-Ranking {{ill|Courtier|en|Courtier|WD=Q1511216|lt=Courtier}} and {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}}. He was the eldest son of {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}}''{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|WD=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}'' {{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/991590/1/39 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}}. His cousin was [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]]. == Career == Like his father Yorimitsu, Yorikuni served primarily as a middle-Ranking official in [[:en:Kyōto|Kyōto]]. He had strong ties to the {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} family, particularly Empress [[:en:Fujiwara no Shōshi|Jōtōmon'in Shōshi]] and her son Prince Atsunari ({{ill|Emperor Go-Ichijō|en|Emperor Go-Ichijō|WD=Q374087|lt=Emperor Go-Ichijō}}). Yorikuni's activities were mainly scholarly rather than martial. Records show his participation in a {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese poetry}} gathering hosted by Michinaga in {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō}} 4 (1007). He was granted [[Naishōden]] privileges and married his daughters to prominent court nobles such as [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] and [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]]. His role in society leaned heavily towards that of a courtier. In legend, Yorikuni is depicted as participating in his father Yorimitsu's famous subjugation of {{ill|Shuten-dōji|en|Shuten-dōji|WD=Q986527|lt=Shuten-dōji}}, alongside the [[Four Heavenly Kings of Yorimitsu]] and [[Fujiwara no Hōshō]]. He is said to have led the attack on the "Oni-ga-jō" (Demon Fortress), the residence of {{ill|Ibaraki-dōji|en|Ibaraki-dōji|WD=Q5983716|lt=Ibaraki-dōji}}. However, historical records only mention Yorikuni's involvement in policing activities around Kyōto, such as the apprehension of bandits in {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kannin}} 3 (1019). An inscription in a ''[[:en:Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra|Mahāprajñāpāramitā Sūtra]]'' at [[Chōshō-ji Temple]] in [[:en:Fuefuki, Yamanashi|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] bears his signature{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/979756/1/10 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=August 3, 2024}} {{Sfn|Yokoyama|2024|p=15}}: {{Quotation|Written in Longyuan 5
For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}} * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Fujiwara no Motohira|en|Fujiwara no Motohira|WD=Q5098972|lt=Fujiwara no Motohira}} * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their {{ill|Waka|en|Waka|WD=Q297934|lt=Waka}} poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]

For the protection of the capital
Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Settsu Province, Minamoto no Asomi
Disciple devoted to the Three Treasures, Yorikuni, humbly inscribes this.}} == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yorimitsu|WD=Q590523|lt=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}} * Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Fujiwara no Motohira|en|Fujiwara no Motohira|WD=Q5098972|lt=Fujiwara no Motohira}} * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nobutaka]] ''' Eldest son: [[Minamoto no Yorihiro]] - Lower Fifth Rank, Governor of Sanuki Province; later renounced the world ''' Third son: [[Minamoto no Yoritane]] (1015–1044) ''' Fourth son: [[Minamoto no Koremichi]] (1020–?) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Moronaga (Governor of Bingo)|Fujiwara no Moronaga]] ''' Second son: [[Minamoto no Yoriyasu]] (?-1066) * Wife: Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Nakaaki]] ''' Fifth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō]] (1025–1097) ''' Eighth son: [[Minamoto no Morimitsu (Seiwa Genji)|Morimitsu]] (?-1100) * Wife: Unknown ''' Sixth son: [[Minamoto no Kunifusa]] (?-1119) ''' Seventh son: [[Minamoto no Yorinaka (Settsu Genji)|Yorinaka]] ''' Ninth son: [[Minamoto no Yoritō (Seiwa Genji)|Yoritō]] ''' Tenth son: [[Minamoto no Tomifusa]] ''' Son: [[Minamoto no Myōen]] ''' Daughter: [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]] (?-1092) ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Akiharu (Onomiya branch)|Fujiwara no Akiharu]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Yoritane]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Sadatsune]] ''' Daughter: Wife of [[Fujiwara no Morotsune]] Yorikuni fathered numerous children. Among them, Yoritane, Yoritō, and Morimitsu were known for their {{ill|Waka|en|Waka|WD=Q297934|lt=Waka}} poetry, while Yoriyasu, Yoritō, Kunifusa, and Morimitsu's descendants became prominent samurai. His daughters included one who became the wife of [[Fujiwara no Ietada]], progenitor of the [[Kazanin family]], and another who married [[Fujiwara no Tamemasa]], ancestor of the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]]. Another daughter, [[Rokujō Saiin Senji]], left her mark as a poet. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|ja |title=The Toki Clan: From the Heian to the Warring States Period |date=April 10, 2024 |publisher=Eibun Kōshō Shuppan |ref=harv |author=Sumio Yokoyama |isbn=978-4-86403-504-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Settsu Genji|ja|摂津源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Genji|1=Settsu Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498578}} * {{ill|Tada Genji|ja|多田源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Genji|1=Tada Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430955}} * {{ill|Mino Genji|ja|美濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Genji|1=Mino Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609245}} * {{ill|Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|ja|丸山城 (摂津国能勢郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Castle (Nose District, Settsu Province)|1=Maruyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368189}} {{translated page|ja|源頼国|version=102979059|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:源頼国]] {{wikidata link|Q11564344}} [[Category:1058 deaths]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Children of Minamoto no Yorimitsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Minamoto Clan of Settsu]] [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Tada Branch of the Minamoto Clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]
